title: Ceramic Sintering Grain Growth Kinetics and Final Density Evolution
content: Al₂O₃ sintering densification: dρ/dt = A·D_gb·γ/(G³·T) where G is grain size and D_gb grain-boundary diffusivity. Grain growth: G² − G₀² = K·t·exp(−Q_g/RT) with Q_g = 480 kJ/mol for alumina. Target: 1550–1600 °C for 2 h yields > 99% theoretical density. Rapid cooling below 1000 °C at ≤ 10 °C/min suppresses abnormal grain growth. Multi-zone kiln enables ≤ ±5 °C uniformity across 500 mm length. Heating rate during binder burnout (300–600 °C) must remain ≤ 1 °C/min to avoid crack formation.
